apply: Apply the concept of symbolism to the novel, specifically the significance of the English countryside
Explain how the author uses foreshadowing to create suspense in the novel
Analyze the character of Ralph Touchett and his role in the novel
Explain how the novel highlights the theme of cultural differences
analyze: Analyze the character development of Isabel Archer throughout the novel
Explain how the novel portrays the concept of freedom and its limitations
Describe the author's use of literary devices such as imagery and metaphor
Analyze the social commentary in the novel and its relevance to the modern world
remember: Define the concept of a 'portrait' and its various forms
Identify and explain the significance of the title 'The Portrait of a Lady'
Recall the main events in the novel 'The Portrait of a Lady'
Explain the character traits of Isabel Archer and Gilbert Osmond
understand: Analyze the role of the protagonist in a novel and its impact on the plot
Explain the theme of self-discovery in the novel
Describe the character of Madame Merle and her relationship with Isabel
Understand the concept of social class and its influence on the novel

ncert_objectives: To understand the plot and characterisation in the novel
To analyze the themes and motifs in the novel
To identify and explain the author's use of literary devices
To evaluate the significance of the novel in the context of literary movements
board_exam_objectives: Identify and explain the theme of the novel
Analyze the character development of the protagonist
Explain the author's use of symbolism and foreshadowing
Evaluate the significance of the novel in the context of literary movements
